_comment: Fresh scenario for the funnel-routing crowding fix (#979): two friendly Cavalry regiments spawn side by side south of the DEFAULT hill (same spawn x positions the default 5v5 battle uses for its own uid8/uid9 -- x=963.95 west of the hill, x=1193.07 inside the hill's own x-range -- see demos/README.md's spawn table), each given an explicit MOVE order to a point just north of the hill (y=350, clearing the hill's own y-range of 380-580 -- NOT a point inside the hill rect itself, which silently no-ops a right-click move order). Unlike an earlier version of this demo (which let the default battle's enemy AI drive both units via a shared, continuously-re-targeted ATTACK order, and used a much farther destination), this version uses a short, fixed MOVE order so each unit's funnel corner is recomputed against a STABLE target every tick and the march completes quickly -- isolating the funnel-corner lane-offset mechanism from two compounding factors that made the demo's own defect-scan margins knife-edge-sensitive: the AI's live-target-chasing behavior, and a periodic formation-compression transient that recurred throughout a much longer march (verified separately: a bare, unfixed funnel corner -- FUNNEL_LANE_SEPARATION_FRACTION temporarily zeroed -- reproduces the underlying bug on this exact scenario, with uid0 stalling for hundreds of ticks around y~750-810 while uid1 completes cleanly). drill mode (no enemy needed -- this demonstrates routing, not combat). World coordinates; physics ticks (60/s).
